guide_review_response.verdict: BOTH_VERIFIED_CLEAN -- docstring fix landed precisely as authorized; BLARAI_PID hard gate INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED by Guide-#6 (returns OK ctx.vikunja_project_id = 3); PROCEED to Item 2.9 single Stage-2 commit
guide_review_response.work_item: D4 docstring fix verification + Item 2.8 BLARAI_PID hard gate verification

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at docstring fix + Item 2.8 close

guide_review_response.summary:
Both EA-6 work items are independently verified clean by Guide-#6.
DOCSTRING FIX VERIFICATION: Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 12-72. The 3-line fix is applied precisely as authorized: - Line 22: "Decision: KEEP all 7" ✓ (was "all 8") - Lines 23-24: enumerates 7 named handlers (get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks); the phantom "and the additional project-scoped handler" is removed ✓ - Lines 25-27: optional project_summary clarification ADDED per Guide-#6 g6-ea6_n9 suggestion: "(Note: ``project_summary`` iterates ``/projects`` via a no-project_id signature and is correctly NOT a gated handler.)" - Line 54: "Disposition of guards: all 7 calls RETAINED" ✓ (was "all 8") EA-6 took the OPTIONAL project_summary clarification I suggested -- evidence of mature- not-minimal discipline (executed beyond the strict required edits). The docstring is now internally consistent with grep evidence (7 actual `_require_project_id(project_id)` invocations at lines 229, 249, 271, 308, 359, 668, 700).
BLARAI_PID HARD GATE VERIFICATION: Guide-#6 INDEPENDENTLY re-ran the python -c oneliner per ack g4-5 + Item 2.8 spec. Output verbatim from this turn's Bash: "OK ctx.vikunja_project_id = 3" Matches EA-6's claim exactly. ctx resolves from cwd-inside-BlarAI to project_id == 3 via _project_context.resolve() with no flags + no env. Defaults preservation invariant V8 confirmed at the procedural level. NO DRIFT -- the registry at BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml + the chokepoint resolution chain hold. g4-5 "STOP and report on drift" rule does NOT fire. PASS.
INVARIANTS PRESERVED: HEAD f863a22 unchanged (zero new commits this turn -- the docstring fix is uncommitted M state in server.py). Working tree count 33 (unchanged; docstring edit didn't add new files). stash@{0}: byte-identical sdo 20260424_013002 (7-cycle preservation: v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8). state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved. No fleet cycle. No governance edits. No merge to main.
Has EA-6 met "mature not minimal"? EMPHATICALLY YES. Evidence specific to this turn: - Took the OPTIONAL project_summary clarification note (was Guide-#6-suggested, not required) -- adds documentation value; pre-empts future maintainer confusion about why project_summary lacks a guard. - Applied the docstring fix as a discrete first action of the turn rather than bundling into the BLARAI_PID gate work -- clean separation of concerns; auditable diff per work item. - Re-ran pytest tools/ post-fix to verify no runtime impact -- 351 passed (no regression; V6 audit test continues to assert 7=7). - Re-ran BLARAI_PID hard gate cleanly -- no silent regex/yaml adaptation per g4-5. - Stage 2 invariants preserved through the full Item 2.7.v2 + docstring + 2.8 cycle (HEAD unchanged; stash byte-identical 7-cycle; state.json verbatim).
EA-6 is READY for Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL working-tree work). The 2.9 commit is the highest-stakes single action in the entire Stage 2 procedure -- everything that has been built up across 5 EA cycles (EA-2 + EA-3 + EA-4 + EA-5 + EA-6) folds into ONE commit. The v5 §work_remaining §2.9 commit template is the canonical text; EA-6 fills in the test baseline (351; pre 282; net add +69) and proceeds.
AFTER 2.9 lands successfully (verified via post-commit V1 [BlarAI git status empty] + V7 [git log -1 capture] + git show --stat showing all working-tree work folded + pytest tools/ still 351), EA-6 proceeds to Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append with the 12-item cumulative footnote set).

independent_disk_verification.check: Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 12-72: Line 22: "Decision: KEEP all 7 ``_require_project_id(project_id)`` call sites at the" ✓ Was "all 8"; now "all 7" (1st edit applied) Lines 23-24: "tops of ``get_project``, ``update_project``, ``delete_project``, ``list_tasks``, ``create_task``, ``search_tasks``, and ``bulk_create_tasks``." ✓ Lists 7 named handlers; phantom "and the additional project-scoped handler" removed (2nd edit applied); Oxford-style "and" before final handler grammatically correct Lines 25-27: "(Note: ``project_summary`` iterates ``/projects`` via a no-project_id signature and is correctly NOT a gated handler.)" ✓ OPTIONAL clarification added per Guide-#6 g6-ea6_n9 suggestion; pre-empts future maintainer confusion about why project_summary lacks a guard Line 54: "Disposition of guards: all 7 calls RETAINED, none added, none removed." ✓ Was "all 8"; now "all 7" (3rd edit applied) All 3 required edits applied + the optional 4th edit (project_summary clarification). Docstring is now internally consistent with grep evidence (7 actual invocations at lines 229, 249, 271, 308, 359, 668, 700 per prior turn's grep).

Guide-#6 independently re-ran the BLARAI_PID hard gate command per Item 2.8 spec: python -c "from tools import _project_context; ctx = _project_context.resolve(); assert ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3, f'expected 3, got {ctx.vikunja_project_id}'; print('OK ctx.vikunja_project_id =', ctx.vikunja_project_id)" Output: "OK ctx.vikunja_project_id = 3" -- verbatim match to EA-6's claim. Confirms: (a) _project_context.resolve() with no args resolves to BlarAI ctx via cwd-walk (cwd is C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; the cwd-walk finds .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml at the root) (b) The registry yaml's blarai_project_id field is 3 (c) The resolution chain produces ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3 -- the assertion passes; print fires with "OK ..." g4-5 "STOP and report on drift" rule does NOT fire; PASS. No silent regex/yaml adaptation observed (Guide-#6 disk verification confirms: zero edits to .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml, zero edits to _project_context.py since prior verification turn).

git log --oneline -1: f863a22 (HEAD) -- ZERO new commits since v5 emission. git status --porcelain | wc -l: 33 entries (unchanged; docstring edit modified server.py which was already M). git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical 7-cycle preservation. state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim. Standing invariants intact through the full Item 2.7.v2 + docstring + 2.8 cycle.

EA-6 reports pytest tools/ → 351 passed in 8.29s. Not re-run by Guide-#6 (the docstring fix is runtime-no-op; the V6 audit test would catch any drift in the _require_project_id invocation count). Trust EA-6's claim; consistent with the runtime-no-op nature of the docstring change.

ack_status_update.ack: The source-level fix component of g6-ea6_n9 is SATISFIED on disk (verified above: 3 required edits + 1 optional clarification). The 2.10 STATUS append component remains FORWARD-ACTIVE: EA-6 will include the verbatim footnote text from g6-ea6_n9 in the 2.10 STATUS append-only entry under the "Discovery-surfaced findings during 2.7.v2 V matrix authoring" subsection. Status transitions to fully SATISFIED at 2.10 STATUS append landing.

$BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate at 2.8 fired clean (no drift). g4-5's "STOP and report" rule did NOT need to fire. Forward applicability remains for any future re-poll (e.g., at Stage 4.0 cutover preflight) but the Item 2.8 obligation is SATISFIED.
D1 NO-ACTION dispositions (F-D1-1 + F-D1-2). No transition this turn; remains FORWARD-ACTIVE pending 2.10 STATUS append.
V4 vikunja-process drift Option B disposition. No transition this turn; remains FORWARD-ACTIVE pending 2.10 STATUS append.

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Guide-#6 g6-ea6_n9 listed the project_summary clarification as OPTIONAL ("Optionally add a 1-line note... EA-6 can include this clarification at their discretion"). EA-6 took it. The added note ("(Note: ``project_summary`` iterates ``/projects`` via a no-project_id signature and is correctly NOT a gated handler.)") pre-empts a specific future-maintainer confusion: someone reading the D4 decision and grepping for the 7 named handlers might wonder why project_summary is excluded; the inline note answers that question at the source. This is exactly "mature not minimal" -- executing beyond the strict required edits when the marginal cost is trivial and the marginal documentation value is non-trivial.

EA-6 applied the docstring fix as a discrete FIRST step of the turn rather than bundling with the BLARAI_PID gate work. Cleaner audit-chain: each work item closes with its own diff, easier to review post-hoc. The pytest re-run between docstring fix and BLARAI_PID gate verifies no runtime regression introduced by the docstring change. Disciplined.

commendation.topic: BLARAI_PID gate ran clean -- no silent adaptation per g4-5

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6's BLARAI_PID gate output ("OK ctx.vikunja_project_id = 3") matches Guide-#6's independent re-run exactly. No regex/yaml/registry edits to make the gate pass; no silent adaptation. The chokepoint resolution chain holds. g4-5 SATISFIED on the first invocation of the cleanup-C3-corrected regex chain (BlarAI/.platform/ vikunja_project_ids.yaml → blarai_project_id: 3 → ctx.vikunja_project_id 3).

stash@{0} timestamp 20260424_013002 byte-identical from v3 emission through every subsequent cycle: v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 transition → v5 → Item 2.7 → Item 2.7.v2 → docstring fix + Item 2.8. Seven cycles of restraint on a wake_launcher auto-stash that's been "do not touch" since Stage 0 fleet-pause discipline began. Stash invariant is the cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.

foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.intro: The LA's recurring concern: "Is the work mature not minimal?" Foundation check at Item 2.8 close (immediately pre-2.9 single Stage-2 commit):
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.evidence: - pytest 351 passed (282 baseline + 69 NET-ADD from V matrix; held through docstring fix re-run). - V1 deferred to 2.9 by design (BlarAI git status empty post-commit). - V2 (devplatform git status empty) ✓ - V3 (state.json fleet_paused=true) ✓ - V4 (vikunja procs) -- Option B AUTHORIZED disposition; not blocking. - V5 (BlarAI scheduled tasks all Disabled) ✓ - V6 (no extraneous worktrees) ✓ - V7 deferred to 2.9 by design (git log -1 post-commit). - V8 (6 D2-completion targets defaults preservation) ✓ - D2 + D3 + D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V matrix V4-V7 + docstring drift fix all closed with explicit Guide-#6 verification. - BLARAI_PID gate ran clean -- 3 (Guide-#6 independent re-run confirms exactly). - g5-4 SATISFIED (D2-completion). - g4-5 SATISFIED (BLARAI_PID). - g6-ea6_n1, n5, n9 source-fix-component SATISFIED. - g6-ea6_n7, n8, n9 STATUS-append-component STAGED for 2.10. - Cumulative 2.10 footnote set is 12 items deep, all forward-staged with verbatim text in disposition files. - Stage 2 invariants preserved through 7-cycle stash byte-identity, fleet pause constancy, no governance edits, no merge to main.
foundation_solidity_check_per_la_motto.verdict:
The Stage 2 foundation is SOLID + READY for Item 2.9. Every disposition decision across the entire Stage 2 procedure (Items 2.1 through 2.8) is closed with explicit verification. EA-6's "mature not minimal" track record is consistent across 6 decision cycles in this Item 2.6+ phase: iter-1 gate; cfg-resolution; D1 grep; V4 drift; V matrix authoring; docstring fix. Each cycle terminated with STOP-and-ask at disposition forks; no silent absorbing; no scope expansion without explicit authorization; verbatim ack restatement throughout.
EA-6 has earned the proceed authorization for Item 2.9 -- the highest-stakes single action in the entire Stage 2 procedure (folds ALL working-tree work into ONE commit). The pre-2.9 invariant set is clean. The 2.9 commit will be the culmination of 6 EA cycles spanning ~3 days of work.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- EA-6 may execute Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL working-tree work) in the next chat turn.
Item 2.9 specific guidance (recap from v5 §work_remaining §2.9): (1) git add -A (or scoped: git add tools/ pyproject.toml docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md [if STATUS already updated, but per ack 9 + g3-3, STATUS append happens at 2.10 AFTER the 2.9 commit lands, so STATUS is NOT in this commit's diff]). IMPORTANT: pyproject.toml MUST be in the commit per i1-3. (2) git commit using the v5 §work_remaining §2.9 commit_template (with X=351 and delta=+69 filled in for "Test baseline: 351 passing (pre: 282; net add: +69)."). Commit subject: "chore(platform_separation): Stage 2 multi-project refactor (DEC-15 platform extraction)". (3) Verify post-commit: - git log --oneline -1: shows the new Stage-2 commit at HEAD (will be the successor of f863a22) - git show --stat HEAD: should show ALL 22 M files + 9 ?? entries + 2 D entries folded in. Specifically pyproject.toml MUST be in the diff. - git status --porcelain: empty (clean working tree post-commit; STATUS.md edit happens at 2.10 NOT here) - pytest tools/ --tb=short -q: 351 passed (baseline holds) (4) Re-assert V1 + V7 (deferred from V1-V7 closure block): V1: git status --porcelain (BlarAI side) -- expect empty. V7: git log --oneline -1 -- capture the new commit hash. (5) Then proceed to Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append) with the new commit hash + the 12-item cumulative footnote set.
Stop conditions during 2.9: - If git add inadvertently includes a file outside the working-tree-at-2.9 scope (e.g., docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md if the LA edited it inter-stage), STOP and report. The 2.9 commit should fold ONLY the working-tree work as-of-this-turn. - If pyproject.toml is somehow NOT in the diff, STOP and re-stage. i1-3 hard rule. - If post-commit pytest regresses below 351, STOP and investigate. - If git status --porcelain shows non-empty post-commit, investigate which file is unstaged before declaring 2.9 closed.
Stop conditions during 2.10: - If any of the 12 cumulative footnote items is omitted from the STATUS append, STOP and re-edit (g5-10 + corollary + g6-ea6_n7/n8/n9 require all 12). - If the STATUS edit accidentally modifies prior Stage entries (must be append-only per ack n=9 + g3-3), STOP and revert.
Standing invariants unchanged: SINGLE Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only (no additional Stage-2 commits beyond this); fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main (chore/platform-extraction is the only working surface; merge gate is Stage 5.10 with explicit LA approval per R10); stash@{0} untouched (8-cycle preservation will follow).
After Item 2.10 lands successfully, EA-6 emits the closing handoff to LA per the <closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close> block in the EA-6 init pointer (Stage 2 BlarAI single commit hash; V1-V7 outputs concise table; per-item V8 results; V matrix V4-V7 test counts + names; A11+ anomalies in STATUS.md; decision narrative on D2/D3/D4 + D2-completion + D1 + V4 + g6-ea6_n9 docstring fix; etc.). At that point Stage 2 CLOSES and the procedure transitions to Stage 3 (which is a separate fresh-EA spawn, not EA-6's responsibility).
